articles.title: India bonds under pressure as state debt supply swells, oil stays elevated
articles.excerpt: Indian government bonds saw a decline early Tuesday due to substantial state debt supply nearing the financial year's end and oil prices remaining above $100 a barrel. States are set to raise a record 584.20 trillion rupees, while Brent crude hovers near $103…

articles.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
articles.excerpt: After Australia gave humanitarian visas to seven team members, only two decided to stay - the BBC's Katy Watson explains what happened.
articles.content:
After Australia granted seven Iranian football players humanitarian visas to stay in the country, only two have decided to stay. 
According to human rights activists, those who left feared reprisals…
articles.author: BBC News

articles.title: UAE repels Iranian missiles and drones as air defence systems engage targets
articles.excerpt: UAE air defence is intercepting missiles and drones from Iran. A tanker near Fujairah sustained minor damage from an unknown projectile. A drone strike ignited a fire at the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone. Emergency teams are working to control the blaze.
articles.content: UAE air defence is intercepting missiles and drones from Iran. A tanker near Fujairah sustained minor damage from an unknown projectile. A drone strike ignited a fire at the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone. Emergency teams are working to control the blaze.
articles.author: ANI

articles.title: Senior Party and Government Officials Vote in Election of SPA Deputies
articles.excerpt: Senior Party and government officials went to sub-constituencies in different parts of the DPRK to take part in the election of deputies to the 15th Supreme People's Assembly together with voters on March 15.
